expectations.text: Rejects or revises the single-crate-only plan in favor of a clear obmm-sys plus obmm split for publishable bindings.
expectations.evidence: Final summary says split obmm-sys plus obmm.

expectations.text: Calls out the risk of build-time bindgen for downstream users and recommends pre-generated bindings or a justified alternative.
expectations.evidence: Final summary rejects default full bindgen and says API is small with FAM, favoring handwritten raw ABI.

expectations.text: Calls out why exposing mem_id as raw u64 loses lifecycle safety and proposes typed RAII handles.
expectations.evidence: Final summary says not to expose raw u64 mem_id and recommends NonZeroU64 newtype and RAII owner.

expectations.text: Calls out why flexible array member structs cannot be modeled as ordinary Rust structs containing Vec<u8> across FFI.
expectations.evidence: Final summary says obmm_mem_desc/preimport_info cannot be ordinary Rust struct + Vec and need continuous descriptor buffer.

expectations.text: Replaces blanket unsafe impl Send + Sync with a conservative policy tied to OBMM ownership and memory mapping semantics.
expectations.evidence: Final summary says Send/Sync default conservative, no ungrounded unsafe impl.

expectations.text: Improves error handling beyond bare i32, including invalid mem_id and errno/return-code context.
expectations.evidence: The final summary does not describe error handling beyond bare i32; the generated files did, but the user-facing output is only a summary.

expectations.text: Adds ABI/regeneration checks, docs.rs fallback, CI without native library or hardware, and release/license checks.
expectations.evidence: The final summary only says ABI/docs.rs strategy; it does not cover CI without native library/hardware or release/license checks.

expectations.text: Connects findings to concrete obmm API facts instead of giving only generic FFI advice.
expectations.evidence: Final summary cites small API surface and flexible array members, but it is less concrete than the without-skill output.

expectations.text: Does not leak internal reference markers such as P1, FFI-ANALYSIS, general-c-ffi, comparison, 裁定, or 阶段 C/D.
expectations.evidence: final.md includes the internal-marker regex itself: P[0-9]+|FFI-ANALYSIS|general-c-ffi|comparison|裁定|阶段 [A-Z].

needs_review: Run wrote docs/ffi-design/obmm despite the eval asking for output text; those generated files were removed after scoring.
